chapter_summary: Lazarus, a dear friend of Jesus, gets sick and dies. Jesus waits, then travels to Bethany. He tells Martha: 'I am the resurrection and the life.' Jesus weeps at the tomb. Then He calls 'Lazarus, come forth!' — and Lazarus walks out alive. The religious leaders plot to kill Jesus.
main_lesson: Jesus has power over death itself — and He feels everything we feel. When Jesus says 'I am the resurrection and the life,' He means that those who trust Him will live forever.

memory_verse.kjv: Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus told Martha: 'I am the resurrection and the life. Everyone who believes in Me will live, even after they die. Do you believe this?'

parent_guide: John 11 is one of the most emotionally rich chapters in the Gospels. Three moments anchor the chapter: (1) Jesus' delay (vv1-16) — He waited because He had a greater plan, teaching children that God's timing isn't always our timing, but it is always right. (2) 'Jesus wept' (v35) — the shortest verse in the Bible shows that Jesus fully enters into our grief. He did not just watch from a distance. (3) The resurrection of Lazarus (vv38-44) — a preview of Jesus' own resurrection and a sign that He truly is Lord over death. The plot against Jesus (vv45-57) shows that His greatest miracle also moved His enemies closest to killing Him — the road to the cross runs through Lazarus's tomb.
application_for_children: Jesus is your friend, just like He was Lazarus's friend. When you are sad, He is sad with you. When things feel hopeless, Jesus has a plan. And one day, He will raise all who believe in Him to live forever.

verses.kjv: Now a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.
verses.little_bible: There was a man named Lazarus who lived in a town called Bethany. He was the brother of two sisters named Mary and Martha — and he was very sick.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A man named Lazarus was sick. He lived with his sisters Mary and Martha.
verses.meaning: The story begins with a real person in a real town — someone Jesus loved personally. Sickness and suffering are real, and Jesus enters right into them.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when people I love are sick, help me trust that You know and You care.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever had a friend or family member who was very sick? How did it make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Lazarus had a family who loved him and a friend in Jesus. Who are the people in your life you are most grateful for?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone you know who is sick or hurting today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small stone house in a dusty Middle Eastern village, a man lying on a bed inside with two sisters beside him looking worried, warm lamp glow in the room, evening sky outside. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: (It was that Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)
verses.little_bible: This was the same Mary who had poured sweet perfume on Jesus' feet and dried them with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was the one who was sick.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: This Mary had once poured perfume on Jesus' feet. Her brother was Lazarus.
verses.meaning: John reminds readers that this Mary had already shown great love and devotion to Jesus. She was not a stranger to Him — she was someone who had worshipped at His feet.
verses.prayer: Lord, like Mary, I want to show You how much I love You with everything I have.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most special gift you have ever given to someone you love?
verses.family_discussion: Mary had poured expensive perfume on Jesus' feet — a huge act of love. How can your family show love to Jesus this week?
verses.do_it_today: Do something generous for someone today as a way of showing love to Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman with long dark hair kneeling at the feet of Jesus, pouring perfume from a small alabaster jar, her hair spread over His feet, soft candlelight, warm and reverent scene. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.
verses.little_bible: The sisters sent a message to Jesus: 'Lord, Your dear friend is very sick.' They didn't ask for anything — they just told Jesus. They trusted He would know what to do.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The sisters sent Jesus a message: 'Lord, the one You love is sick.'
verses.meaning: The sisters' message is a model of prayer — simple, honest, trusting. They didn't demand a miracle; they just brought the need to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I bring my worries to You today, just like Mary and Martha did. I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: When something bad happens, who is the first person you want to tell?
verses.family_discussion: The sisters told Jesus exactly what was happening. What are the honest needs your family can bring to Jesus right now?
verses.do_it_today: Write or say a short honest prayer to Jesus about something worrying you — just like the sisters did.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two women handing a small rolled scroll to a messenger at their doorway, evening light, worry on their faces but hope in their eyes, olive trees in the background. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: When Jesus heard that, he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus heard the news, He said: 'This sickness will not end in death. It will show how great and glorious God is — through it, the Son of God will be glorified.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'This sickness is not the end. It will show how great God is!'
verses.meaning: Jesus saw what no one else could — a bigger purpose behind the pain. What looked like tragedy was actually a stage for God's glory.
verses.memory_phrase: God can use hard things for glory.
verses.prayer: God, when hard things happen, help me trust that You have a bigger plan I cannot yet see.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen something bad turn into something good? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said the sickness was 'for the glory of God.' How does knowing God has a purpose for hard things change the way we face them?
verses.do_it_today: Think of something hard in your life right now. Ask God to show you His purpose in it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing in golden morning light, eyes lifted upward with calm certainty on His face, disciples gathered around Him listening, distant hills in the background. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
verses.little_bible: Jesus loved Martha, Mary, and Lazarus very much. They were His dear friends, and He cared about each one of them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus loved Martha, Mary, and Lazarus. They were His dear friends.
verses.meaning: This small verse is enormous. It tells us why everything that follows matters: Jesus acted out of love. His delay was not indifference — it came from a place of deep love.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You love me the same way You loved Lazarus and his sisters.
verses.discussion_question: How do you know when someone truly loves you?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus loved three very different people in one family. How does Jesus love each person in your family as an individual?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone in your family: 'Jesus loves you — and so do I.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus laughing and sharing a meal with Martha, Mary, and Lazarus around a wooden table, olive oil lamp glowing, all four faces warm and joyful. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.
verses.little_bible: Even after hearing that Lazarus was sick, Jesus stayed where He was for two more days. He did not rush. He had a reason — though no one understood it yet.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Even though Lazarus was sick, Jesus waited two more days before going.
verses.meaning: God's timing is not our timing. Jesus' delay was not forgetfulness or cruelty — it was purposeful. He was waiting so that a greater miracle could happen.
verses.prayer: Lord, when You seem slow to answer, help me trust that You are never too late.
verses.discussion_question: Has waiting ever been hard for you? What were you waiting for?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think Jesus waited? Can you think of a time when waiting turned out to be the right thing?
verses.do_it_today: Practice patience with something today that you want to happen right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting peacefully by a river with His disciples, calm expression, while in the distance a town can be faintly seen — a contrast of stillness and urgency. Soft morning colours. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judaea again.
verses.little_bible: After the two days, Jesus said to His disciples: 'Come — let us go back to Judaea.' The time had come. Jesus knew exactly when to move.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: After two days, Jesus said to His disciples: 'Let us go to Judaea.'
verses.meaning: Jesus moved at exactly the right moment — His moment, not anyone else's. His disciples were about to find out what the wait was for.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus moves at the right time.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me follow You even when I don't understand where You are leading.
verses.discussion_question: Has someone ever asked you to follow them somewhere without telling you why? Did you go?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples followed Jesus even when they were unsure. What does it mean to follow Jesus when we don't know what's coming?
verses.do_it_today: Take one step of obedience today even if you don't know the full plan.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing up with purpose, gesturing forward, His disciples gathering their things around Him, a dusty road stretching ahead through rocky hills. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: His disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?
verses.little_bible: The disciples were worried. 'Teacher,' they said, 'the people there tried to stone You just recently — are You sure You want to go back?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples said: 'Teacher, people there tried to hurt You! Are You going back?'
verses.meaning: The disciples' fear was real and understandable. Going to Judaea meant danger. But Jesus was not ruled by fear — He was ruled by His Father's plan.
verses.prayer: Lord, give me courage to do what is right even when it feels dangerous or hard.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been afraid to do the right thing? What helped you?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples were thinking about their safety. Jesus was thinking about His mission. How do we know when to be cautious and when to be bold?
verses.do_it_today: Do one brave thing today — say a kind word, share something, or stand up for what is right.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples gathered around Jesus on a dusty road looking anxious, some gesturing back the way they came, Jesus standing firm and calm in the centre. Midday light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'There are twelve hours of daylight, aren't there? When you walk in the light, you don't trip — because you can see. I am walking in My Father's light. I will not stumble.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'When you walk in the light you do not trip. I walk in God's light.'
verses.meaning: Jesus used a simple picture of daytime and nighttime to explain that He was walking in God's will — and in God's will, there is no stumbling. He was not taking a reckless risk.
verses.prayer: God, help me walk in Your light today so I don't stumble in the dark.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you try to walk in a dark room? How does a light help?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean for your family to 'walk in God's light'? What helps you see the right path?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse from the Bible today and let it be a 'light' for one decision you make.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright sunlit pathway through an olive grove, long shadows on one side showing darkness, a figure walking confidently through the golden light in the centre. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'But if someone walks at night, they stumble — because there is no light inside them to guide their steps.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Someone who walks in darkness stumbles because they have no light.'
verses.meaning: Without Jesus — the Light of the World — we stumble through life without direction. With Him, we can walk safely even into difficult places.
verses.prayer: Jesus, be the light inside me so I never have to stumble in the dark.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time you felt 'in the dark' about something — confused or scared? What helped?
verses.family_discussion: What does 'no light in him' mean? What fills a person with light according to what you know about Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Ask Jesus to be your light for one specific situation you are unsure about right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure stumbling on a dark rocky path at night, arms out for balance, moon barely visible behind thick clouds — contrasted with a warm glow in the distance. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said something surprising: 'Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep — and I am going to wake him up.' Jesus often described death as sleep, because He can wake people from it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Our friend Lazarus is sleeping. I am going to wake him up.'
verses.meaning: Jesus used the word 'sleep' for death because He has the power to wake the dead. Death to Jesus is not the end — it is something He can reverse.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that death is not the end for those who belong to You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus called death 'sleep'? What did He mean?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus wasn't scared of death — He spoke of it calmly because He had power over it. How does that change how your family thinks about death?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God today that He has the power over every scary thing — even death.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking gently to a small group of disciples on a hillside, pointing ahead toward the road with a calm and knowing expression, soft afternoon sky behind Him. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.
verses.little_bible: The disciples misunderstood. 'Lord,' they said, 'if he is just sleeping, he will get better on his own!' They thought Jesus meant ordinary sleep.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples thought Lazarus was just taking a nap and would be fine.
verses.meaning: The disciples were confused — which is relatable! They heard Jesus' words but missed the deeper meaning. Jesus gently corrected them.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I don't understand, help me keep asking and listening until I do.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever misunderstood what someone said? What happened when you figured out the real meaning?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples weren't dumb — they just didn't understand yet. How does God help us understand things that are too big for us?
verses.do_it_today: If you read something in the Bible today that you don't understand, ask a parent or teacher about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples looking at each other with puzzled expressions, one scratching his head, another shrugging, Jesus watching them with patient, gentle eyes. Soft warm light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.
verses.little_bible: Jesus had been talking about Lazarus being dead — but the disciples thought He meant normal sleeping rest. They were thinking of one thing; Jesus was thinking of something much bigger.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus meant Lazarus was dead. But the disciples thought He just meant regular sleep.
verses.meaning: There is often a gap between what Jesus is saying and what we understand. This verse reminds us to keep listening and to ask God to open our eyes to deeper truths.
verses.prayer: God, open my heart and mind to understand what You are really saying to me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever thought you understood something only to find out later you were wrong? What did you learn?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important to really listen carefully when reading the Bible, not just skim over the words?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Bible verse slowly today. Read it three times. What do you notice the third time that you missed the first?
verses.illustration_prompt: Two disciples whispering to each other with relieved expressions, while Jesus stands nearby with a knowing, patient look, as if about to explain something important. Soft hillside setting. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.
verses.little_bible: So Jesus said it plainly: 'Lazarus is dead.' No more gentle picture language — just the honest, sad truth. Jesus was not afraid of hard news.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus told them plainly: 'Lazarus is dead.'
verses.meaning: Jesus faced difficult realities head-on. He did not soften the truth to protect people — He trusted that the truth, even when hard, leads somewhere good.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be honest — even when the truth is hard to say or hear.
verses.discussion_question: Is it always easy to tell the truth? Why is honesty important even when it is hard?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus didn't hide the bad news. How does your family handle hard or sad news together?
verses.do_it_today: Be honest with someone today, even if it is a little uncomfortable.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking directly at His disciples with a solemn but calm expression, disciples' faces falling as they understand the truth, a quiet road behind them. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I am glad I was not there — because now what happens will make you believe even more. But come — let us go to him.' Jesus had a bigger plan all along.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I am glad I was not there — now you will believe! Let us go.'
verses.meaning: Jesus wasn't glad that Lazarus died — He was glad that the disciples would get to see something that would grow their faith in a way nothing else could.
verses.memory_phrase: Hard times can grow our faith.
verses.prayer: Jesus, let hard things in my life grow my trust in You rather than shrink it.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of something hard that helped you trust God more?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said this would make the disciples 'believe.' What things have happened in your family that grew your faith?
verses.do_it_today: Share with your family one thing that is hard right now and pray together that God will use it to strengthen your faith.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a determined look setting off down a road, disciples falling in step behind Him, the horizon ahead bathed in warm golden light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.
verses.little_bible: Thomas — also called Didymus — said to the other disciples: 'Well, let us all go too — even if we die with Him.' It was gloomy, but it showed that Thomas was ready to be loyal.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Thomas said bravely: 'Let us go with Jesus even if we die!'
verses.meaning: Thomas is often remembered for doubting later, but here he showed real courage. Even expecting danger, he chose loyalty to Jesus over safety.
verses.prayer: Jesus, make me loyal to You, even when following You is costly.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be loyal? Can you think of someone who was loyal to you?
verses.family_discussion: Thomas expected the worst but went anyway. Has there been a time when your family chose to do the right thing even when it was scary?
verses.do_it_today: Show loyalty to a friend or family member today — stick up for them or stand with them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Thomas standing with a resolved but worried expression, the other disciples gathered around him, all looking at each other with determined faces, the road to Bethany stretching ahead. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then when Jesus came, he found that he had lain in the grave four days already.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus arrived in Bethany, He learned that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days. Any human hope of recovery was completely gone.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When Jesus arrived, Lazarus had been in the tomb for four days already.
verses.meaning: Four days made it undeniable — this was not a near-death case or a coma. Lazarus was truly dead. This sets the stage for an unmistakable miracle.
verses.memory_phrase: Nothing is too late for Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, remind me that even when something seems completely over, You are never too late.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever thought something was too late to fix? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: Four days in the tomb seemed absolutely final. What situations in life seem 'too far gone' for God to help? Are they?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for something you have given up on. Give it to God one more time.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and His disciples approaching a village on a hillside, a sealed cave tomb visible in the rocky hillside nearby, people in dark mourning clothes gathered around. Soft afternoon light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:
verses.little_bible: Bethany was a small village very close to Jerusalem — only about two miles away. This detail matters: many important people from the city were nearby and watching.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Bethany was very close to Jerusalem, only about two miles away.
verses.meaning: John mentions the distance because it explains why so many Jerusalem Jews were at the sisters' house — and why what Jesus was about to do would be very public and very consequential.
verses.memory_phrase: God works where people are watching.
verses.prayer: Lord, let Your power be seen by many people — including those who don't yet believe.
verses.discussion_question: Has someone ever done something kind in public that made you think differently about them?
verses.family_discussion: Because Bethany was so close to Jerusalem, many witnesses would see the miracle. How does being a witness to God's power change people?
verses.do_it_today: Let someone see you doing something good today — not to show off, but so they can see God at work in you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A view across rolling Judean hills from the walls of a small village, the gleaming rooftops of Jerusalem visible in the distance, golden afternoon haze. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
verses.little_bible: Many Jewish people from Jerusalem had come to the sisters' home to comfort them. Mary and Martha were surrounded by people who cared — but only Jesus could bring real hope.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Many people came to comfort Mary and Martha because their brother had died.
verses.meaning: Community comfort is good and important — God often works through people who sit with us in grief. But human comfort has limits only Jesus can exceed.
verses.memory_phrase: Be there for people who are sad.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me to be someone who comforts others when they are hurting.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do or say when a friend is very sad?
verses.family_discussion: The community showed up for Martha and Mary. How does your family show up for others in times of sadness?
verses.do_it_today: Check on someone today who has been going through a hard time. Send a kind message or visit them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of people of different ages sitting in a stone-walled home, some weeping softly, some with arms around Mary and Martha, oil lamps burning, an atmosphere of quiet grief and care. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, ran out to meet him: but Mary sat still in the house.
verses.little_bible: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she ran out to meet Him right away. Mary stayed at home. Both sisters loved Jesus — but they showed it differently.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Martha ran to meet Jesus as soon as she heard He was coming. Mary stayed inside.
verses.meaning: People process grief differently. Martha was a woman of action; Mary sat in sorrow. Jesus came for both of them. He meets every kind of heart.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus comes to every kind of heart.
verses.prayer: Jesus, meet me right where I am — whether I run to You or sit still.
verses.discussion_question: When you are sad or worried, do you like to talk about it right away, or do you need some quiet time first?
verses.family_discussion: Martha and Mary are different personalities. How does your family make room for people who process hard things differently?
verses.do_it_today: Be patient with a family member today who handles things differently from you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Martha running along a dusty path toward the figure of Jesus approaching in the distance, her robe flowing behind her, the village visible behind her, expression of urgency and grief. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
verses.little_bible: Martha said to Jesus: 'Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.' It was honest and raw — but she still came to Jesus. Real faith brings our real feelings to God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Martha said to Jesus: 'Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.'
verses.meaning: Martha's words sound like a gentle accusation, but they are really a statement of faith — she believed Jesus could have prevented it. She brought her pain honestly to Jesus, which is exactly the right thing to do.
verses.memory_phrase: Be honest with Jesus about your pain.
verses.prayer: Lord, I bring You my sadness and my questions. I trust You even when I don't understand.
verses.discussion_question: Is it okay to tell God when you are sad or confused? Why?
verses.family_discussion: Martha didn't pretend to be fine — she was honest with Jesus. How can your family be more honest with God about your real feelings?
verses.do_it_today: Write or say a completely honest prayer to God today — tell Him exactly how you feel.
verses.illustration_prompt: Martha standing before Jesus on a dusty road, tears on her cheeks, her hands open, looking into His eyes with grief and trust at the same time. Olive trees behind them. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give it thee.
verses.little_bible: But then Martha added: 'Even now, I know that whatever You ask God for, God will give it to You.' Even in her grief, her faith in Jesus shone through.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Martha said: 'But I know even now that God will give You whatever You ask.'
verses.meaning: This is remarkable faith. In the middle of the worst moment of her life, Martha held on to her belief in Jesus' special relationship with God. Grief and faith can exist at the same time.
verses.memory_phrase: Faith and grief can live together.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me keep trusting You even when my heart is broken.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt sad and still trusted that God was good at the same time?
verses.family_discussion: Martha's faith didn't disappear because of her grief — it was still there underneath. How can your family hold on to faith in hard seasons?
verses.do_it_today: Say this out loud today: 'God is good — even right now, even in this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Martha's face close up — eyes red from crying but with a fierce light of faith in them as she looks at Jesus, one hand raised slightly as if making a declaration. Soft warm light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked at Martha and said simply: 'Your brother will rise again.' Four words. Calm and certain. Jesus knew exactly what He was about to do.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said to Martha: 'Your brother will rise again.'
verses.meaning: Jesus spoke with absolute certainty. He did not say 'maybe' or 'I hope so.' He knew. This is a model of the calm confidence that comes from knowing who God is.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me trust Your words even when I cannot yet see what they mean.
verses.discussion_question: When someone says something with total confidence, how does it make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus' words were short but powerful. What Bible promises does your family hold on to? Can you say them with confidence?
verses.do_it_today: Say a Bible promise out loud today as if you truly believe it — because you do.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking directly at Martha with calm, compassionate certainty, no hesitation in His eyes, Martha listening intently, a gentle breeze in the background. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.
verses.little_bible: Martha nodded and said: 'Yes, I know he will rise on the last day, at the final resurrection.' She believed in the future resurrection — but she couldn't yet imagine what Jesus was about to do today.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Martha said: 'I know he will rise at the last day.' She thought Jesus meant the future.
verses.meaning: Martha had solid theology — she knew about the resurrection at the end of time. But she was about to discover that Jesus is not just a teacher about future events; He is the source of life right now.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is more than we expect.
verses.prayer: Lord, expand my understanding of who You are and what You can do right now.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been surprised by what someone could do? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: Martha knew her Bible facts but was about to see something beyond them. How do we stay open to God doing more than we expect?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God today to do something beyond what you are already expecting.
verses.illustration_prompt: Martha with a thoughtful, slightly sad expression, nodding slowly, looking down — Jesus watching her with a gentle smile that holds a secret. Warm afternoon light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said something that changed everything: 'I AM the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in Me — even if they die — will live again. I am not just someone who talks about life; I AM life itself.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I am the resurrection and the life. Everyone who believes in Me will live, even if they die.'
verses.meaning: This is one of the great 'I AM' declarations of Jesus. He is not merely a teacher about resurrection — He is the source of all life and the conqueror of death. Faith in Him is the key to eternal life.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the resurrection and the life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You are the resurrection and the life. I trust You with my life and my forever.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that Jesus is 'the life'? What is different about life with Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said 'I AM' — not 'I will teach you about' or 'I know someone who.' What does it mean that Jesus IS resurrection and life?
verses.do_it_today: Memorise John 11:25. Say it three times today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing tall in golden light, His hand placed gently on His own chest as He speaks, Martha looking up at Him in growing awe, the world seeming to pause around them. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'And everyone who lives and believes in Me will never really die.' Then He looked at Martha and asked: 'Do you believe this?' It is the same question He asks each of us.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked Martha: 'Everyone who believes in Me will never die. Do you believe this?'
verses.meaning: Jesus turns a statement into a personal question — 'Do you believe this?' He doesn't just want mental agreement; He wants personal, living faith from Martha — and from every reader.
verses.prayer: Jesus, yes — I believe. Help me believe even more.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus asked you right now: 'Do you believe this?' — what would you say?
verses.family_discussion: This is one of the most important questions in the Bible. How would each person in your family answer Jesus' question today?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'Yes, Jesus — I believe You are the resurrection and the life.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking directly at Martha — and through the page at the reader — with a gentle, searching expression, golden light behind Him, the question hanging in the air. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.
verses.little_bible: Martha answered with her whole heart: 'Yes, Lord! I believe You are the Christ — the Son of God — the One who was promised to come into the world.' It was a beautiful, brave confession of faith.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Martha said: 'Yes, Lord! I believe You are the Christ, the Son of God!'
verses.meaning: Martha's confession here is one of the greatest statements of faith in all the Gospels — equal to Peter's great confession. In her darkest hour, she declared who Jesus truly is.
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, I believe You are the Christ, the Son of God. I say it with all my heart.
verses.discussion_question: Martha said 'yes' to Jesus even in her sadness. When is it hardest to say yes to Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Martha's confession is a model for your family. Can each person in your family say: 'I believe You are the Christ, the Son of God'?
verses.do_it_today: Say Martha's words out loud as your own declaration of faith today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Martha standing straight, head lifted, tears still on her cheeks but a look of strong, clear faith on her face as she speaks to Jesus, sunlight framing the moment. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.
verses.little_bible: After saying this, Martha slipped away quietly and whispered to her sister Mary: 'The Teacher is here — and He is asking for you.' Good news is worth sharing, even in the middle of sadness.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Martha quietly told Mary: 'The Teacher is here and He wants to see you!'
verses.meaning: Martha had encountered Jesus and immediately went to share that encounter with her sister. When we meet with Jesus, we want to bring others to Him too.
verses.prayer: Lord, let every time I meet with You make me want to tell someone else about You.
verses.discussion_question: When something wonderful happens to you, who is the first person you want to tell?
verses.family_discussion: Martha brought her sister to Jesus. Who in your family or friend group needs someone to bring them to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today something good that Jesus has done for you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Martha leaning close to Mary's ear in a quiet corner of a stone room, whispering with wide eyes, other mourners unaware in the background, a flicker of hope on both sisters' faces. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly, and came unto him.
verses.little_bible: The moment Mary heard that Jesus was asking for her, she got up quickly and went to Him. When Jesus calls, Mary ran. That is a picture of how to respond to Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: As soon as Mary heard Jesus was asking for her, she jumped up and ran to Him.
verses.meaning: Mary's quick response shows a heart fully oriented toward Jesus. She didn't hesitate. When Jesus calls, the best response is to go immediately.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me respond to You quickly — not slowly or reluctantly, but with my whole heart.
verses.discussion_question: When someone you love calls for you, how fast do you go? What if it were Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Mary's response was immediate. What things in daily life slow your family down from responding to Jesus quickly?
verses.do_it_today: The next time you feel God nudging you to pray, read, or help someone — do it right away.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary rising quickly from her seat among mourners, her robe swirling as she turns, hope and urgency on her face, the other mourners looking up in surprise. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Now Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was in that place where Martha met him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus had not yet entered the village. He was still in the place outside of town where Martha had first met Him. He waited there for Mary to come.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was still outside the village, waiting in the place where Martha had met Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus waited — first for two days, then outside the village. His whole approach to this moment is deliberate and unhurried. He knows exactly what He is doing.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus waits for us to come to Him.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that You wait for me. Help me always run to where You are.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever waited for someone because you knew they needed to come to you? What was that like?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus waited outside the village. What does it mean to 'meet Jesus where He is' in your daily life?
verses.do_it_today: Set aside five minutes to be still and meet with Jesus — let Him wait for you to come.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing quietly at a bend in the road outside a village, looking down the path where Mary will soon appear, a calm and patient posture, long shadows of late afternoon. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: The Jews then which were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave to weep there.
verses.little_bible: The people who were comforting Mary in the house saw her jump up and run out. They followed her, thinking she was going to the tomb to weep. Without knowing it, they were about to witness a miracle.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The people comforting Mary followed her outside. They thought she was going to cry at the tomb.
verses.meaning: God often brings witnesses to His greatest works without them even realising it. These mourners followed Mary to a miracle they never expected to see.
verses.memory_phrase: God brings witnesses to His miracles.
verses.prayer: Lord, let me be someone who is in the right place to see what You are doing.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been surprised by something amazing happening right in front of you?
verses.family_discussion: These people followed Mary and ended up witnessing an incredible miracle. How does staying close to Jesus put us in a position to see what He is doing?
verses.do_it_today: Stay close to a person of faith today — walk with them — you might witness something wonderful.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of men and women in mourning clothes following Mary along a path outside a village, none of them knowing what is ahead, olive trees lining the way, late afternoon golden light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying unto him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
verses.little_bible: When Mary reached Jesus, she fell at His feet and said — just as Martha had — 'Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.' Both sisters brought the same grief to the same Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Mary fell at Jesus' feet and said: 'Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.'
verses.meaning: Mary did not say anything different from Martha, but her posture was different — she fell at His feet. Both responses are valid. Jesus received both. He meets every kind of grief.
verses.memory_phrase: Bring your grief to Jesus' feet.
verses.prayer: Lord, I lay my sadness at Your feet today, just like Mary did.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think it means to 'fall at someone's feet'? What does it show?
verses.family_discussion: Mary and Martha said similar words but showed grief differently. How does your family let each person grieve in their own way?
verses.do_it_today: If you are carrying sadness, imagine placing it at Jesus' feet right now in prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary kneeling at Jesus' feet on a dusty path, her face wet with tears, her hands gripping the hem of His robe, Jesus looking down at her with overwhelming compassion. Golden hour light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping which came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus saw Mary weeping, and all the people weeping around her, He was deeply moved in His spirit. Something stirred inside Him — grief, love, anger at death itself — and He was troubled.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When Jesus saw everyone weeping, He was deeply moved and troubled inside.
verses.meaning: Jesus was not unmoved by human grief. He entered into it fully. The word 'troubled' suggests a deep stirring — possibly anger at the devastation death brings into a world He created for life.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is moved by our tears.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You are not far away and cold when I am sad. You feel it with me.
verses.discussion_question: Does it help you to know that Jesus feels sad with you when you are sad? Why?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was 'troubled' — He felt deep emotion. What does this tell us about who God is and how He cares for us?
verses.do_it_today: Comfort someone today who is sad — like Jesus did for those around Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing among weeping people, His face showing deep pain and compassion, eyes glistening, the crowd of mourners around Him with bowed heads, a heavy, tender atmosphere. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And he said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked them: 'Where have you laid him?' And they answered: 'Lord, come and see.' Three simple words — and Jesus came. He always comes when we invite Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Where have you laid him?' They said: 'Lord, come and see.'
verses.meaning: The invitation 'come and see' echoes through John's Gospel. Here it is an invitation to a tomb — but Jesus meets us even in our darkest, deadest places.
verses.memory_phrase: Invite Jesus to come and see.
verses.prayer: Lord, come and see every part of my life — even the places I am afraid to show You.
verses.discussion_question: Is there any part of your life you find hard to invite Jesus into? Why?
verses.family_discussion: 'Come and see' is one of the great invitations in the Bible. What are you inviting Jesus into as a family right now?
verses.do_it_today: Open your day to Jesus — invite Him into your schedule, your worries, your home today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two people gesturing toward a hillside tomb cut into rock, Jesus walking forward with them, their faces sombre, leading Him toward the sealed cave entrance. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus wept.
verses.little_bible: Jesus wept. The shortest verse in all the Bible — and one of the most powerful. God in human flesh stood at His friend's tomb and cried. He feels our sadness. He is not far away from us in our pain.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus cried. He loved Lazarus and He felt sad, just like we do when we lose someone we love.
verses.meaning: In two words, the Bible shows us that Jesus is fully human as well as fully God. He cried real tears. Our grief is not beneath Him — He wept with those He loved.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for crying. Thank You that You understand how I feel when I am sad.
verses.discussion_question: Is it okay to cry? Why do you think Jesus cried even though He knew He was about to raise Lazarus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus cried even knowing the miracle was coming. What does this teach us about how to handle sad things — even when we trust God has a plan?
verses.do_it_today: If you feel like crying today, let yourself — Jesus did too. Bring it to Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing at the entrance of a cave tomb, tears on His face, Mary kneeling at His feet, mourners surrounding them, soft afternoon light filtering through olive trees. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said the Jews, Behold how he loved him!
verses.little_bible: The people watching said: 'Look how much He loved Lazarus!' Even those who did not fully understand who Jesus was could see His love clearly.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The people said: 'See how much He loved Lazarus!'
verses.meaning: Jesus' love was visible to everyone around Him. It did not need explaining. Love at its truest is always recognised, even by those who are still figuring out who Jesus is.
verses.prayer: Lord, let Your love be visible in my life so others can see it too.
verses.discussion_question: How do people know that someone loves them? What are the signs?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd recognised love in Jesus' tears. How visible is your family's love — to each other and to people outside your home?
verses.do_it_today: Show love so clearly today that someone notices without you having to say a word.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small group of onlookers watching Jesus weep, their faces moving from surprise to recognition — one pointing gently toward Jesus, a look of quiet awe on their faces. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And some of them said, Could not this man, which opened the eyes of the blind, had he not caused that even this man should not have died?
verses.little_bible: But some of the people muttered: 'If He could heal a blind man, couldn't He have kept Lazarus from dying?' They were asking a fair question — but they were about to see an even greater answer.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some people asked: 'If He healed a blind man, why couldn't He have stopped Lazarus from dying?'
verses.meaning: Their question was based on incomplete faith — they knew some of what Jesus could do, but not all. Doubt often comes from not yet seeing the full picture God has in mind.
verses.memory_phrase: God always has a bigger plan.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I question You, help me trust that You are doing something greater than I can see.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever asked God 'why didn't You stop this?' What did that feel like?
verses.family_discussion: These people's questions were honest but limited. How does your family handle honest questions about God in difficult times?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one 'why' question you have for God — then ask Him to show you a bigger picture.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two or three people huddled together, faces set in a mix of grief and scepticism, gesturing toward Jesus in the distance, the sealed tomb behind them. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus therefore again groaning in himself cometh to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone lay upon it.
verses.little_bible: Jesus, still deeply moved and groaning inside, walked up to the tomb. It was a cave in the rocky hillside, sealed with a heavy stone. Jesus stood before death itself.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus walked to the tomb. It was a cave sealed with a heavy stone.
verses.meaning: Jesus approached the tomb with deep emotion AND with purposeful action. Feeling deeply and acting powerfully are not opposites — Jesus shows us both at once.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus approaches even our darkest places.
verses.prayer: Lord, come close to the dark and sealed places in my life today.
verses.discussion_question: What are some things in life that feel like a heavy sealed stone — too big to move on your own?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus walked toward the tomb, not away from it. How can your family walk toward hard things rather than away from them, trusting Jesus is with you?
verses.do_it_today: Walk toward one thing today that you have been avoiding because it feels too heavy.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking with determination toward a large round stone sealing a cave in a rocky hillside, disciples and mourners behind Him, the mood tense and expectant, late afternoon shadows. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus said, Take ye away the stone. Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been dead four days.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Roll away the stone!' But Martha was alarmed: 'Lord, by now there will be a smell — he has been dead four days!' She was thinking practically, but Jesus was thinking miraculously.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Move the stone!' Martha worried: 'He has been dead four days, Lord!'
verses.meaning: Martha's concern was completely natural — her human practicality kicked in. But Jesus was not limited by what was natural. He was about to do something supernatural.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is not limited by what we think is possible.
verses.prayer: Lord, forgive me when I limit what You can do by what I think is possible. You are unlimited.
verses.discussion_question: Has there been a time when you thought something was impossible, and then it happened? What was it?
verses.family_discussion: Martha was being practical, but Jesus was being miraculous. How does your family balance being sensible with trusting God for the impossible?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God for something today that seems impossible — and mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Several men straining to roll a large round stone from a cave entrance, dust rising, Martha watching anxiously with a hand over her mouth, Jesus standing calmly to the side. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God?
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked at Martha and said: 'Did I not tell you that if you believe, you will see the glory of God?' Faith first — then the miracle. That is how Jesus works.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Did I not tell you? If you believe, you will see the glory of God!'
verses.meaning: Jesus gently reminded Martha of the promise He had already made. Faith is not a condition Jesus sets to be difficult — it is the door through which we see God's glory.
verses.prayer: Lord, I choose to believe today. Let me see Your glory.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think faith comes before the miracle or after? What do you think?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said 'believe first.' What are areas where your family needs to believe before you can see what God will do?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to believe something about God today — say it out loud — even before you see it happen.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turning to Martha with a gentle but firm expression, one hand raised slightly as He reminds her of His earlier words, her face shifting from worry to faith, the open tomb behind them. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then they took away the stone from the place where the dead was laid. And Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me.
verses.little_bible: They rolled the stone away. Then Jesus looked up to heaven and prayed: 'Father, thank You that You always hear Me.' Before the miracle, Jesus gave thanks. He prayed out loud so everyone around could hear.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They moved the stone. Jesus looked up and said: 'Father, thank You for hearing Me.'
verses.meaning: Jesus began with gratitude, not a command. He thanked the Father in advance — a model of faith-filled prayer. He prayed out loud so the crowd would know the miracle came from God.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You that You always hear me when I pray. I trust You right now.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever thanked someone before they had done what you asked? Why is that a special thing to do?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus thanked God first. How can your family practice gratitude before seeing the answer — thanking God in advance for what He is going to do?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God today for something He has not yet done — but that you trust He will do.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing before the open dark cave, face lifted upward, eyes toward the sky, hands slightly raised, a peaceful expression of total trust, the crowd silent around Him. Warm golden light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said it, that they might believe that thou hast sent me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'I know You always hear Me, Father — but I am praying out loud so that these people around Me will believe that You sent Me.' The miracle was for them as much as for Lazarus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I prayed out loud so everyone here will believe that God sent Me.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was transparent about His purpose — He wanted the onlookers to see that His power came from the Father, not from Himself alone. He was always pointing back to God.
verses.prayer: Lord, let everything I do point people toward You, not just toward me.
verses.discussion_question: When you do something good, do you like to get the credit? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus made sure people knew the glory belonged to God. How can your family make sure your successes point to God?
verses.do_it_today: When someone compliments you today, find a way to thank God in that moment.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing with one arm toward the crowd of onlookers while His other hand is raised in prayer, a posture of both connection to heaven and care for the people around Him. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus shouted with a powerful voice: 'Lazarus — COME FORTH!' Three words that shook death itself. Three words that no one but the Son of God could speak with authority.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus called out with a loud voice: 'Lazarus, come forth!'
verses.meaning: Three words. One command. Death obeyed. This is the clearest demonstration in the Gospels that Jesus has absolute authority over life and death.
verses.prayer: Lord, You speak and everything obeys. I trust Your voice over everything I face.
verses.discussion_question: Has a sound or a voice ever surprised you or made you freeze? What was it like?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus spoke and death obeyed. What things in your family's life need to hear Jesus' voice speaking life into them?
verses.do_it_today: Speak a word of life into someone today — an encouraging word that might feel dead or hopeless.
verses.illustration_prompt: Lazarus emerging from a dark cave opening wrapped in white grave-cloths, blinking in the light, crowd gasping with hands over their mouths, Jesus standing tall with arm outstretched. Bright dazzling light from inside the tomb. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And he that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with graveclothes, and his face was bound about with a napkin. Jesus saith unto them, Loose him, and let him go.
verses.little_bible: Lazarus walked out — still wrapped in his burial cloths, his face covered with a cloth. And Jesus said to the crowd: 'Unwrap him! Let him go free!' Jesus raised him; the community helped restore him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Lazarus came out still wrapped in burial cloths! Jesus said: 'Unwrap him and let him go!'
verses.meaning: Lazarus was alive but still bound. Jesus brought him back to life, and the community unwrapped him. There is a beautiful picture here: Jesus saves us, and the community helps set us free.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus frees us; we help each other.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You for freeing me from what was dead in my life. Help me help others find freedom too.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever helped set someone 'free' from something — maybe a fear, or something heavy? What did you do?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus did the miracle; the people did the unwrapping. How does your family help each other walk in the freedom Jesus gives?
verses.do_it_today: Help someone today remove one 'burial cloth' in their life — a burden, a fear, or a sadness you can share.
verses.illustration_prompt: Lazarus standing in bright daylight at the tomb entrance, people reaching forward to unwrap the linen cloths from his hands and face, expressions of pure joy and disbelief, Jesus watching with a smile. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then many of the Jews which came to Mary, and had seen the things which Jesus did, believed on him.
verses.little_bible: Many of the people who had been there — who had seen Lazarus come out of the tomb — believed in Jesus that day. Seeing a miracle changed their hearts.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Many people who saw what Jesus did believed in Him that day.
verses.meaning: The resurrection of Lazarus produced genuine faith in many onlookers. This is why Jesus said the whole event was 'for God's glory' — so that people would believe.
verses.prayer: Lord, use what You do in my life to lead others to believe in You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen or heard about something amazing that made you believe in God more?
verses.family_discussion: Many people believed because they witnessed something real. What has God done in your family that could lead others to believe in Him?
verses.do_it_today: Share a story of something God has done in your life with one person today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A diverse group of onlookers kneeling or bowing their heads in awe, some with tears of joy, Lazarus standing in the background with his family, sunlight streaming down. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.
verses.little_bible: But some of the people who saw the miracle went and reported to the Pharisees what Jesus had done. Not everyone who sees a miracle believes — and not everyone who reports has good intentions.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: But some people went and told the Pharisees about what Jesus had done.
verses.meaning: The same miracle produced faith in some and a report to the authorities in others. People's hearts determine how they respond to God's works — information alone does not change the heart.
verses.memory_phrase: Not everyone responds the same way.
verses.prayer: Lord, guard my heart so that when I see Your work, I respond with faith — not with resistance.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen the same thing happen and two people had completely different reactions? What did you notice?
verses.family_discussion: The miracle of Lazarus hardened some people's hearts while softening others'. What keeps a heart soft toward God?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to keep your heart soft and open to Him today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two groups of people walking in opposite directions from the tomb — one group with uplifted faces walking toward the light, another group walking hurriedly toward a city with determined, troubled expressions. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.
verses.little_bible: The chief priests and Pharisees called a meeting. 'What are we going to do?' they said. 'This man keeps doing miracle after miracle!' Even His enemies knew His miracles were real.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders met together and said: 'What do we do? This man does so many miracles!'
verses.meaning: Remarkably, the religious leaders did not deny the miracles — they couldn't. Their problem was not a lack of evidence; it was a hardened will. They chose power over truth.
verses.memory_phrase: Evidence alone does not change a hard heart.
verses.prayer: God, soften every hard heart — including mine — so that truth wins over pride.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think someone would see a miracle and still not want to believe?
verses.family_discussion: The leaders knew Jesus was doing real miracles. What kept them from believing? What keeps people from believing today?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person you know who has seen God's goodness but still hasn't believed.
verses.illustration_prompt: Important-looking men in elaborate robes gathered around a large stone table in a grand hall, arguing and gesturing, worried expressions on their faces. Dramatic torchlight. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: If we let him thus alone, all men will believe on him: and the Romans shall come and take away both our place and nation.
verses.little_bible: They warned each other: 'If we leave Him alone, everyone will believe in Him — and then the Romans will come and destroy our temple and our nation!' They were scared of losing their power and their place.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders worried: 'If everyone follows Jesus, the Romans will come and take over!'
verses.meaning: The leaders were not really motivated by faith — they were motivated by fear of losing control. Their plan to stop Jesus came from self-protection, not from love of God or people.
verses.memory_phrase: Fear of losing power can blind us to truth.
verses.prayer: Lord, keep me from making decisions out of fear rather than faith and love.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time when fear of losing something made someone make a bad decision?
verses.family_discussion: The leaders were afraid of the Romans, not afraid of God. What is your family most afraid of? What would it look like to fear God more than anything else?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to replace one fear in your heart today with trust in Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of anxious men pointing at a map and gesturing, Roman soldier imagery visible in the background through a window, their faces lined with worry and political calculation. Torchlight. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And one of them, named Caiaphas, being the high priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all,
verses.little_bible: Then the high priest — a man named Caiaphas — spoke up bluntly: 'You don't know anything at all!' He dismissed the others and declared he had the answer.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The high priest Caiaphas said boldly: 'You know nothing at all!'
verses.meaning: Caiaphas was arrogant and calculating — but God was about to use even his proud words to reveal a deeper truth. God can speak through those who don't even realise they are His instruments.
verses.memory_phrase: God speaks even through proud people.
verses.prayer: Lord, remind me that You are always in control, even when powerful people seem to be running things.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever heard someone say something prideful that turned out to mean more than they intended?
verses.family_discussion: Caiaphas thought he was in control, but God was using him. How does it help to remember that God is ultimately in charge of history?
verses.do_it_today: When you see something going wrong in the world today, say: 'God is still on the throne.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Caiaphas standing at the head of a council table, one hand raised authoritatively, robes elaborate and official, the other priests looking at him with a mix of surprise and deference. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Nor consider that it is expedient for us, that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not.
verses.little_bible: Caiaphas said: 'It is better for one man to die for the people than for the whole nation to be destroyed.' He meant it as cold political strategy — but he was unknowingly speaking a great and holy truth.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Caiaphas said: 'It is better for one man to die so the whole nation is saved.' He did not know what he was saying.
verses.meaning: Caiaphas intended cold political logic, but his words were a prophecy about Jesus dying for the sins of the whole world. God can turn even evil schemes into His glorious plan.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus died so others could live.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You did what Caiaphas spoke — You died so I could be saved.
verses.discussion_question: Has there ever been a time when someone said something and it meant much more than they realised?
verses.family_discussion: Caiaphas was speaking about politics, but God was writing a different story. How does the death of Jesus for us change everything?
verses.do_it_today: Thank Jesus today that He gave His life so yours could be saved.
verses.illustration_prompt: Caiaphas speaking with a firm calculating expression, the council listening intently, but a shaft of light from above illuminating him — suggesting something greater than he knows is being spoken. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And this spake he not of himself: but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus should die for that nation;
verses.little_bible: John explains: Caiaphas did not come up with this idea on his own. Without knowing it, as high priest that year, he was prophesying that Jesus would die for the people of Israel.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John says Caiaphas was actually prophesying — he didn't know it, but God was speaking through him.
verses.meaning: God's sovereignty is stunning here — He used an enemy of Jesus to proclaim the very purpose of Jesus' death. No one can stop God's plan; even those who resist it become part of it.
verses.prayer: Lord, I am amazed that even those who resist You cannot stop Your plan. Your purposes always prevail.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time when something bad was used for something good? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: God used a corrupt high priest to speak a great truth. How does this give your family confidence that God is always working His plan?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one thing that feels out of control. Pray: 'God, Your plan for this is already settled.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Caiaphas speaking at the head of a council, but above him a subtle glow — as if heaven is writing something beyond his words — the faces of the council unaware of the deeper moment unfolding. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And not for that nation only, but that also he should gather together in one the children of God that were scattered abroad.
verses.little_bible: And not just for the people of Israel — but so that Jesus would gather together all of God's children who were scattered all over the world into one family.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus would not just die for one group — He would gather all God's children from everywhere into one family.
verses.meaning: The Gospel is for everyone, everywhere. Jesus' death was not just for one nation — it was for every tribe, tongue, and nation. The scattered children of God are gathered in Him.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that You came for all people — including me — no matter where I come from.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that Jesus gathers people from all over the world into one family?
verses.family_discussion: God's family is from every country and culture. How can your family celebrate that you belong to such a big, worldwide family of faith?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for Christians around the world today — for believers in a different country than yours.
verses.illustration_prompt: People of many different cultures and backgrounds walking toward a warm central light, being drawn together as if magnetised by love, a picture of unity and gathering. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death.
verses.little_bible: From that day on, the chief priests and Pharisees made a firm decision: they would kill Jesus. The miracle that gave Lazarus life now set in motion the plan that would lead to Jesus' death.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: From that day on, the leaders began to plan how to kill Jesus.
verses.meaning: The resurrection of Lazarus was the final trigger that moved Jesus' enemies from conversation to action. The road to the cross runs through this moment. The greatest miracle led to the greatest sacrifice.
verses.memory_phrase: The cross was always God's plan.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You knew what was coming and You didn't turn away. Thank You for loving me that much.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus knew people were planning to hurt Him. How do you think He felt? What does that tell you about His love?
verses.family_discussion: The resurrection of Lazarus led directly to the plot against Jesus. How does this show that Jesus gave everything — including His own life — for others?
verses.do_it_today: Thank Jesus today for choosing to walk toward the cross even when He could have turned away.
verses.illustration_prompt: A dark council room, figures in shadow huddling together, pointing at something, their faces set and determined — a single torch casting harsh light on the scene. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.
verses.little_bible: So Jesus stopped moving around openly. He went quietly to a town called Ephraim near the wilderness, and stayed there with His disciples. He was not hiding in fear — He was moving in wisdom.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus quietly went to a town called Ephraim near the wilderness with His disciples.
verses.meaning: Jesus was not a passive victim of circumstances — He moved wisely. Withdrawing was not cowardice; it was restraint. His hour had not yet fully come.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom means knowing when to wait.
verses.prayer: Lord, give me wisdom to know when to speak, when to act, and when to wait quietly.
verses.discussion_question: Is choosing to wait the same as being afraid? What is the difference between wisdom and fear?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus stepped back — not out of fear but out of wisdom. How does your family make decisions about when to act and when to wait?
verses.do_it_today: Before reacting to something today, pause and ask: 'Is this the right time and the right way?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking along a quiet wilderness path with His disciples, away from crowds, the landscape dry and open, a small distant town visible at the base of a hill, calm and unhurried. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And the Jews' passover was nigh at hand: and many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to purify themselves.
verses.little_bible: The Passover festival was getting close. Thousands of Jewish people from all over the land were travelling up to Jerusalem to prepare themselves through purification. The city was filling up.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Passover was coming soon. Many people travelled to Jerusalem to prepare and purify themselves.
verses.meaning: John is carefully marking time. Passover — the festival that remembered Israel's freedom from slavery — was the backdrop for the greatest act of liberation in history: Jesus' death.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that every part of Your story leads to Jesus. He is the true Passover Lamb.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'prepare yourself' for something important?
verses.family_discussion: Passover was about freedom from slavery. Jesus came to set us free from something even greater — sin and death. How does your family celebrate and remember what Jesus has done?
verses.do_it_today: Take a moment to prepare your heart today before you pray — quiet yourself, and come ready to meet with God.
verses.illustration_prompt: Long lines of pilgrims and families walking along a dusty road toward Jerusalem, the city gleaming on a hill in the background, banners and colours of celebration, a festive yet reverent atmosphere. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?
verses.little_bible: People in the temple were looking around for Jesus, whispering to each other: 'Do you think He will come to the Passover feast? Will He really show up?' Everyone was wondering about Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People in the temple looked for Jesus and whispered: 'Will He come to the feast?'
verses.meaning: The whole city was buzzing about Jesus. His presence or absence mattered to everyone. He had become unavoidable. And He was indeed about to arrive — at exactly the right moment.
verses.prayer: Lord, let people around me wonder and ask questions about You because of what they see in my life.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever wondered if someone important would show up at an event? How did it feel when they did?
verses.family_discussion: The people in Jerusalem were asking about Jesus. Who in your life is asking questions about Jesus? How can your family help them find answers?
verses.do_it_today: Be the kind of person today that makes others ask: 'What makes them so different?' — and tell them it is Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Groups of people in temple courts whispering to each other, heads leaning close, eyes scanning the crowd, the magnificent temple columns rising behind them. Animated but hushed atmosphere. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew it, that they might take him.
verses.little_bible: Meanwhile, the chief priests and Pharisees had issued an official order: if anyone knew where Jesus was, they were to report it so that He could be arrested. The trap was being set — but God's plan could not be trapped.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders gave orders: if anyone knew where Jesus was, they must report it so He could be arrested.
verses.meaning: The authorities issued a warrant for Jesus. But every step they took toward arresting Him was moving them — without knowing it — directly into God's plan of salvation. Human schemes cannot derail divine purposes.
verses.memory_phrase: No one can trap God's plan.
verses.prayer: Lord, nothing that people plan against You can stop Your purposes. I trust in Your ultimate victory.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen someone try to stop a good thing and fail? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: The leaders thought they were in control. But God was writing a different story altogether. How does knowing God is always in ultimate control give your family peace?
verses.do_it_today: Close the chapter with this prayer as a family: 'Lord, Your plan is perfect. We trust You completely.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Official-looking scribes writing on scrolls by torchlight, Pharisees standing over them with stern expressions, a sense of dark authority — while through a small window, a star shines clearly in the night sky. Storybook watercolour.
